concept_name: The Biot-Savart Law — Magnetic Field of a Current Element
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism §4.4 (Biot-Savart Law) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Pedagogy SEQUENCE sourced (structure only, never prose) from a Physics Wallah lecture 'Moving Charges and Magnetism 01: Biot-Savart Law' per AUTHORING_PIPELINE.md Stage 1. The atomic concept being taught is the GENERAL law dB = (μ₀/4π) I(dl × r̂)/r² for a single current element, and how summing every element along a straight wire RECOVERS the familiar circular field B = μ₀I/(2πr). This is the most-confused derivation in Class 12 magnetism: students think dB points along the current, treat every element as contributing equally, and never see that the circular field they memorised is built (integrated) from countless tiny perpendicular contributions. The field_3d renderer makes the element, the cross-product direction, and the accumulation visible in 3D.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the main coil of an MRI machine in a hospital — a tube wound with current-carrying wire. The huge, steady magnetic field it places around the patient is not produced by some single magic source. It is the sum of the tiny fields of millions of short wire segments, each one a current element obeying the Biot-Savart law. Master the field of ONE element and you understand the field of every coil, magnet, and motor ever built.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture a single straight power-supply wire in your home inverter. To find the magnetic field it makes at a nearby point, you cannot use the whole wire at once — you mentally chop it into tiny pieces, find the field of each piece with Biot-Savart, and add them up. The familiar B = μ₀I/2πr is the answer to that addition, not a separate rule.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a circular current loop, like a single turn of an electromagnet. The field at its centre is found the same way: break the loop into current elements, apply Biot-Savart to each, and sum. Every magnetic field in the world is just dB contributions added up — the Biot-Savart law is the atom of magnetostatics.

r.name: Perpendicular distance from wire to field point P

theta.name: Angle between the current element dl and the unit vector r-hat to P

dB_magnitude.formula: (mu_0 * I * dl * sin(theta)) / (4 * PI * r * r)

formulas.direction: dB is along dl × r̂ — perpendicular to BOTH the current element and the line to P (tangent to the circle through P)
formulas.integrated_straight_wire: Summing dB over an infinite straight wire gives B = μ₀I/(2πr)

constraints: dB is the field of ONE infinitesimal current element; the total field at P is the vector sum (integral) over all elements of the wire
Magnitude dB ∝ I, ∝ dl, ∝ sinθ, and ∝ 1/r² (inverse-square in distance)
Direction of dB is dl × r̂ — perpendicular to both dl and r̂, never along the wire and never radially toward P
An element collinear with r̂ (θ → 0 or 180°) contributes zero field at P because sinθ = 0
Integrating Biot-Savart over an infinite straight wire reproduces exactly B = μ₀I/(2πr); over a finite wire it gives B = (μ₀I/4πr)(sinφ₁ + sinφ₂)

aha_moment.statement: The circular field is the sum of one tiny dB from every current element.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Watch the elements switch on one by one; their perpendicular dB contributions add up and the full circle assembles itself.

scene_composition.text: Straight wire: B = μ₀I / 2πr

You met this with
the compass + right-hand rule

But WHERE does
the circle come from?

tts_sentences.text_en: You already know this: a long straight wire carrying current wraps a magnetic field in circles around itself.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप यह पहले से जानते हैं: एक long straight wire जो current carry करती है, अपने चारों ओर magnetic field को circles में wrap करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు ఇది ఇప్పటికే తెలుసు: current carry చేస్తున్న ఒక long straight wire తన చుట్టూ magnetic field ని circles లో wrap చేసుకుంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Its strength is the magnetic field B equals mu-naught I over two pi r. You met it with the compass and the right-hand rule.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसकी strength है magnetic field B equals mu-naught I over two pi r। आप इसे compass और right-hand rule के साथ मिले थे।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని strength magnetic field B equals mu-naught I over two pi r. మీరు దీన్ని compass మరియు right-hand rule తో కలుసుకున్నారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: But here is the real question: WHERE does that circular field come from? Why a circle, and why that exact size?
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन यहाँ असली सवाल है: वह circular field कहाँ से आता है? एक circle क्यों, और बिल्कुल उस exact size का क्यों?
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ ఇక్కడ నిజమైన ప్రశ్న: ఆ circular field ఎక్కడ నుండి వస్తుంది? ఒక circle ఎందుకు, మరియు ఆ exact size ఎందుకు?

tts_sentences.text_en: To answer it, we stop looking at the whole wire and zoom into one tiny piece of it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसका जवाब देने के लिए, हम पूरे wire को देखना बंद करते हैं और उसके एक tiny piece में zoom करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: దానికి సమాధానం చెప్పడానికి, మనం మొత్తం wire ను చూడడం ఆపి దాని ఒక tiny piece లోకి zoom చేస్తాం.

scene_composition.text:
dl — one tiny element
(carries the same current I)
P — a point in space
Chop the wire into countless pieces

tts_sentences.text_en: Chop the wire into countless tiny pieces. Each piece has a small length we call d-l, and it carries the same current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire को countless tiny pieces में chop करें। हर piece की एक small length होती है जिसे हम d-l कहते हैं, और वह same current I carry करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: wire ని countless tiny pieces లో chop చేయండి. ప్రతి piece కి ఒక small length ఉంటుంది, దానిని మనం d-l అని పిలుస్తాం, మరియు అది same current I ని carry చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is one such element, d-l, glowing on the wire. This little arrow points along the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक ऐसा element है, d-l, wire पर glow करता हुआ। यह little arrow current के along point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ అలాంటి ఒక element ఉంది, d-l, wire పై glow అవుతోంది. ఈ little arrow current తో along point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick any point P in space near the wire. We will ask: how much magnetic field does THIS one element create at P?
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire के पास space में कोई भी point P चुनें। हम पूछेंगे: यह एक element P पर कितना magnetic field बनाता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: wire దగ్గర space లో ఏదైనా point P ని ఎంచుకోండి. మనం అడుగుతాం: ఈ ఒక element P వద్ద ఎంత magnetic field సృష్టిస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Biot and Savart answered exactly that. The law is about one element first; we add up all of them later.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Biot और Savart ने exactly यही answer दिया। यह law पहले एक element के बारे में है; हम बाद में सभी को add up करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Biot మరియు Savart exactly దానికే సమాధానం ఇచ్చారు. ఈ law ముందు ఒక element గురించి; మనం వాటన్నింటినీ తర్వాత add up చేస్తాం.

misconception_watch.belief: d-l means the whole wire taken as one source
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Only the short glowing segment lights up, not the whole wire — the field is built by summing every such piece.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: d-l is one infinitesimal piece of the wire; the total field is the sum of the contributions of every piece.

scene_composition.text:
r̂ — direction from dl to P
(distance r)
θ — angle between dl and r̂
Two things fix the field at P: r and θ

tts_sentences.text_en: To describe P's position, draw an arrow from the element d-l straight to the point P. Its length is r, the distance.
tts_sentences.text_hi: P की position describe करने के लिए, element d-l से point P तक straight एक arrow खींचें। इसकी length r है, जो distance है।
tts_sentences.text_te: P యొక్క position ను describe చేయడానికి, element d-l నుండి point P కి straight గా ఒక arrow గీయండి. దాని length r, అది distance.

tts_sentences.text_en: The unit vector along it, r-hat, just gives the direction from the element toward P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसके साथ unit vector, r-hat, बस element से P की तरफ direction देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దానితో unit vector, r-hat, కేవలం element నుండి P వైపు direction ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now notice the angle between the element d-l and this arrow r-hat. Call it theta. This angle will matter a lot.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब element d-l और इस arrow r-hat के बीच के angle पर ध्यान दें। इसे theta कहिए। यह angle बहुत matter करेगा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు element d-l మరియు ఈ arrow r-hat మధ్య angle ని గమనించండి. దీనిని theta అని పిలవండి. ఈ angle చాలా matter చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two things fix the field at P: how far P is, the distance r, and this angle theta. Let us build the formula from them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो चीजें P पर field को fix करती हैं: P कितनी दूर है, distance r, और यह angle theta। इनसे हम formula बनाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: P వద్ద field ని రెండు విషయాలు fix చేస్తాయి: P ఎంత దూరంలో ఉంది, distance r, మరియు ఈ angle theta. వీటి నుండి formula ని రూపొందిద్దాం.

scene_composition.text:
dB ∝ I · dl · sinθ
dB ∝ 1/r²
dB = (μ₀/4π) · I dl sinθ / r²
μ₀/4π = 10⁻⁷ T·m/A

tts_sentences.text_en: Experiment shows the field d-B from one element grows with the current. More current, more field: d-B is proportional to the current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Experiment दिखाता है कि एक element से field d-B current के साथ बढ़ता है। ज़्यादा current, ज़्यादा field: d-B proportional है current I के।
tts_sentences.text_te: Experiment చూపిస్తుంది field d-B ఒక element నుండి current తో పెరుగుతుంది. ఎక్కువ current, ఎక్కువ field: d-B అనేది current I కి proportional.

tts_sentences.text_en: It also grows with the length of the element and with sine of the angle theta. Longer piece, larger angle, stronger contribution.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह element की length और angle theta के sine के साथ भी बढ़ता है। Longer piece, larger angle, stronger contribution।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది element length తో మరియు angle theta యొక్క sine తో కూడా పెరుగుతుంది. Longer piece, larger angle, stronger contribution.

tts_sentences.text_en: And it weakens with distance, not as one over r, but as one over r squared, an inverse-square fall-off.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और यह distance के साथ कमज़ोर होता है, one over r की तरह नहीं, बल्कि one over r squared की तरह, एक inverse-square fall-off।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ఇది distance తో బలహీనపడుతుంది, one over r గా కాదు, కానీ one over r squared గా, ఒక inverse-square fall-off.

tts_sentences.text_en: Put them together with the constant mu-naught over four pi. Scalar form: d-B equals mu-naught over four pi, times I d-l sine theta, over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इन्हें constant mu-naught over four pi के साथ मिलाएँ। Scalar form: d-B equals mu-naught over four pi, times I d-l sine theta, over r squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: వీటిని constant mu-naught over four pi తో కలపండి. Scalar form: d-B equals mu-naught over four pi, times I d-l sine theta, over r squared.

misconception_watch.belief: Every element of the wire contributes equally to the field at P
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The proportionality bars shrink for far-off, small-angle elements — sinθ and 1/r² both cut the contribution down.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Contribution scales as sinθ over r squared: the element across from P gives the most, the far collinear ends give almost nothing.

scene_composition.text:
dB ‖ dl × r̂
(⊥ to both)

tangent to the circle —
never along the wire
Right hand: curl dl → r̂, thumb = dB

tts_sentences.text_en: That gave the SIZE of d-B. But a field also has a direction. Which way does d-B point at P?
tts_sentences.text_hi: इससे d-B का SIZE पता चला। लेकिन एक field की direction भी होती है। d-B P पर किस way में point करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: అది d-B యొక్క SIZE ని ఇచ్చింది. కానీ ఒక field కి direction కూడా ఉంటుంది. d-B P వద్ద ఏ way లో point చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The full law is a cross product: d-B is along d-l cross r-hat, perpendicular to BOTH the element and the line to P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पूरा law एक cross product है: d-B, d-l cross r-hat के along होता है, जो element और P तक की line दोनों के perpendicular होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: పూర్తి law ఒక cross product: d-B, d-l cross r-hat తో along ఉంటుంది, element కి మరియు P కి వెళ్ళే line కి రెండింటికీ perpendicular గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Curl your right hand from d-l toward r-hat; your thumb gives d-B. Here it points straight into the page, the cross symbol.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपना right hand d-l से r-hat की ओर curl करें; आपका thumb d-B देगा। यहाँ यह straight page के अंदर point करता है, cross symbol।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ right hand ని d-l నుండి r-hat వైపు curl చేయండి; మీ thumb d-B ని ఇస్తుంది. ఇక్కడ అది straight page లోపలికి point చేస్తుంది, cross symbol.

tts_sentences.text_en: So d-B never points along the wire and never points toward P. It is perpendicular to both, tangent to the circle through P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो d-B कभी wire के along point नहीं करता और कभी P की ओर point नहीं करता। यह दोनों के perpendicular होता है, P से गुजरने वाले circle के tangent होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి d-B ఎప్పుడూ wire తో along point చేయదు మరియు ఎప్పుడూ P వైపు point చేయదు. ఇది రెండింటికీ perpendicular, P గుండా వెళ్ళే circle కి tangent.

tts_sentences.text_en: Quick check before we go on: at a point on the OTHER side of the wire, does d-B point into the page or out of it?
tts_sentences.text_hi: आगे बढ़ने से पहले एक quick check: wire के OTHER side पर एक point पर, क्या d-B into the page point करता है या out of it?
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం ముందుకు వెళ్లే ముందు ఒక quick check: wire కు OTHER side లో ఒక point వద్ద, d-B into the page point చేస్తుందా లేదా out of it?

misconception_watch.belief: d-B points along the current d-l, that is, along the wire
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The d-B arrow at P is drawn perpendicular to the element and to r-hat, lying tangent to the circle — never parallel to the wire.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: d-B is perpendicular to BOTH d-l and r-hat by the cross product; it is tangent to the circle, never along the wire.

scene_composition.text: Apply the rule yourself

· current OUT of page
→ B circles anti-clockwise

⊗ current INTO page
→ B circles clockwise

tts_sentences.text_en: Let us practise the direction rule. When the current comes straight out of the page toward you, we mark it with a dot.
tts_sentences.text_hi: चलिए direction rule का अभ्यास करते हैं। जब current सीधे page से आपकी तरफ out आता है, तो हम इसे एक dot से mark करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం direction rule ను practice చేద్దాం. current నేరుగా page నుండి మీ వైపు out వచ్చినప్పుడు, మనం దానిని ఒక dot తో mark చేస్తాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use d-l cross r-hat for a point to its right: the field circles anti-clockwise, so on the right side d-B points up.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसके right में एक point के लिए d-l cross r-hat का उपयोग करें: field anti-clockwise circles बनाता है, इसलिए right side पर d-B up point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని right లోని ఒక point కోసం d-l cross r-hat ను ఉపయోగించండి: field anti-clockwise గా circles చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి right side లో d-B up గా point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now flip it. Current going INTO the page, marked with a cross. The field circles clockwise instead.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब इसे flip करें। Current INTO the page जा रहा है, एक cross से marked। इसके बजाय field clockwise circles बनाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు దానిని flip చేయండి. Current INTO the page వెళుతోంది, ఒక cross తో marked. దీని బదులుగా field clockwise గా circles చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same cross-product rule every time. The dot and cross are just the current pointing at you or away from you.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर बार same cross-product rule। Dot और cross बस current है जो आपकी तरफ या आपसे दूर point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతిసారి same cross-product rule. Dot మరియు cross కేవలం current, అది మీ వైపు లేదా మీ నుండి దూరంగా point చేస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: d-B points along r-hat, straight toward the point P
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Across all dot and cross cases, the field arrow stays tangent to the circle — it never points radially from the wire to P.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: d-B is perpendicular to r-hat, tangent to the circle, never radially toward P.

scene_composition.text: Add every element's dB → the circle emerges

each element drops
a tiny dB at P

∑ dB = the full
circular field B

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the payoff. The whole wire is millions of these elements. Each one adds its own tiny d-B at P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब payoff। पूरा wire इन millions of elements से बना है। हर एक P पर अपना tiny d-B जोड़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు payoff. మొత్తం wire ఈ millions of elements తో నిండి ఉంది. ప్రతి ఒక్కటి P వద్ద తన tiny d-B ని కలుపుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch them switch on, one after another, up and down the wire. Every element drops a small d-B contribution at P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उन्हें एक के बाद एक, wire के up and down, switch on होते देखें। हर element P पर एक small d-B contribution देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటిని ఒకదాని తర్వాత ఒకటి, wire వెంట up and down, switch on అవుతూ చూడండి. ప్రతి element P వద్ద ఒక small d-B contribution ను అందిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Add them all; that is the integral. The tiny perpendicular pieces line up and build one smooth circular field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: tiny perpendicular pieces line up होते हैं और एक smooth circular field बनाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: tiny perpendicular pieces line up అయ్యి ఒక smooth circular field ను నిర్మిస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The circle you already knew was never assumed. It EMERGES from summing Biot-Savart over every element of the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जिस circle को आप पहले से जानते थे, उसे कभी assume नहीं किया गया था। यह wire के हर element पर Biot-Savart को sum करने से EMERGES होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు అప్పటికే తెలిసిన circle ఎప్పుడూ assume చేయబడలేదు. ఇది wire లోని ప్రతి element పై Biot-Savart ను sum చేయడం ద్వారా EMERGES అవుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Biot-Savart's law and Ampere's law give different magnetic fields
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The assembled circle's size matches B = μ₀I/2πr exactly — the very field Ampere's law gives.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Summing every d-B from Biot-Savart gives exactly B equals mu-naught I over two pi r, the same field Ampere's law gives.

scene_composition.text: Contribution ∝ sinθ / r²

element across from P
(θ = 90°): biggest dB

far ends (θ → 0):
sinθ → 0, dB ≈ 0

tts_sentences.text_en: Not every element pulls its weight. Look at the lengths of the d-B arrows as we walk along the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर element अपना weight pull नहीं करता। wire के साथ चलते समय d-B arrows की lengths को देखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి element తన weight ను pull చేయదు. wire వెంట నడుస్తున్నప్పుడు d-B arrows యొక్క lengths ను చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The element directly across from P, where theta is ninety degrees and r is smallest, gives the biggest contribution.
tts_sentences.text_hi: P के ठीक सामने वाला element, जहाँ theta ninety degrees है और r smallest है, सबसे biggest contribution देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: P కి నేరుగా ఎదురుగా ఉన్న element, ఎక్కడైతే theta ninety degrees మరియు r smallest అవుతుందో, అది biggest contribution ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Far up or down the wire, the element nearly lines up with r-hat. Sine theta shrinks toward zero, so its d-B almost vanishes.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire में बहुत ऊपर या नीचे, element लगभग r-hat के साथ line up होता है। Sine theta zero की तरफ shrink करता है, इसलिए उसका d-B लगभग vanish हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: wire లో చాలా పైకి లేదా క్రిందకి, element దాదాపు r-hat తో line up అవుతుంది. Sine theta zero వైపు shrink అవుతుంది, కాబట్టి దాని d-B దాదాపు vanish అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is why a long wire behaves like an infinite one: the distant ends add almost nothing. The nearby piece dominates.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसीलिए एक long wire एक infinite wire की तरह behave करता है: distant ends लगभग कुछ भी add नहीं करते। Nearby piece dominate करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అందుకే ఒక long wire ఒక infinite wire లా behave చేస్తుంది: distant ends దాదాపు ఏమీ add చేయవు. Nearby piece dominate చేస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: An element's contribution falls off only because of the one-over-r-squared distance term
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The far-end arrows collapse faster than distance alone would predict, because their sinθ is also heading to zero.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: It falls off from BOTH one-over-r-squared AND sine theta; the collinear ends vanish because sine theta goes to zero.

scene_composition.text: Infinite wire: B = μ₀I / 2πr

exactly the formula
you started with

finite wire: B = (μ₀I/4πr)
(sinφ₁ + sinφ₂)

tts_sentences.text_en: Carry out the full sum for an infinite straight wire and the magnitude comes out to mu-naught I over two pi r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक infinite straight wire के लिए full sum करें और magnitude mu-naught I over two pi r आता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక infinite straight wire కోసం full sum చేస్తే, magnitude mu-naught I over two pi r వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Exactly the formula you started with. Biot-Savart did not just agree with it; it EXPLAINED where it comes from.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बिल्कुल वही formula जिससे आपने शुरुआत की थी। Biot-Savart सिर्फ इससे agree नहीं किया; उसने EXPLAIN किया कि यह कहाँ से आता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీరు మొదలుపెట్టిన formula సరిగ్గా అదే. Biot-Savart దీనితో agree అవ్వడమే కాదు; అది ఎక్కడ నుండి వస్తుందో EXPLAIN చేసింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: For a finite wire the answer is mu-naught I over four pi r, times sine phi-one plus sine phi-two, a topic for another day.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक finite wire के लिए answer है mu-naught I over four pi r, times sine phi-one plus sine phi-two, यह किसी और दिन का topic है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక finite wire కోసం answer mu-naught I over four pi r, times sine phi-one plus sine phi-two, ఇది వేరే రోజు కోసం ఒక topic.

tts_sentences.text_en: One law for one element, summed over the whole wire, reproduces every straight-wire field you will ever meet.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक element के लिए एक law, जिसे पूरे wire पर sum किया गया है, हर straight-wire field को reproduce करता है जिससे आप कभी मिलेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక element కోసం ఒక law, మొత్తం wire పై sum చేయబడింది, మీరు ఎప్పుడైనా ఎదుర్కొనే ప్రతి straight-wire field ను reproduce చేస్తుంది.

scene_composition.text: Drag the I and r sliders

Predict before the meter:
bigger I → stronger, bigger r → weaker
One element · one cross-product rule · summed over the wire

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Use the sliders to change the current I and the distance r, and watch the assembled circle respond.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। current I और distance r को change करने के लिए sliders का उपयोग करें, और assembled circle को respond करते हुए देखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ వంతు. current I మరియు distance r ని మార్చడానికి sliders ని ఉపయోగించండి, మరియు assembled circle ఎలా respond అవుతుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move the field point closer and the contributions grow; push it far away and the whole field weakens as one over r.
tts_sentences.text_hi: field point को closer लाएँ और contributions grow करते हैं; इसे far away धकेलें और whole field one over r के रूप में weaken होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: field point ని closer కి జరపండి మరియు contributions grow అవుతాయి; దానిని far away కి నెట్టండి మరియు whole field one over r లా weaken అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict before you read the meter: bigger I means stronger field, bigger r means weaker. The shape stays a circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: meter पढ़ने से पहले predict करें: bigger I का मतलब stronger field, bigger r का मतलब weaker field। The shape एक circle ही रहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: meter చదివే ముందు predict చేయండి: bigger I అంటే stronger field, bigger r అంటే weaker. The shape ఒక circle లాగే ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: One element, one cross-product rule, summed over the wire. That is the Biot-Savart law, and that is where the magnetic field B comes from.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक element, एक cross-product rule, जिसे wire पर sum किया गया। यह Biot-Savart law है, और magnetic field B यहीं से आता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక element, ఒక cross-product rule, wire పై sum చేయబడింది. అది Biot-Savart law, మరియు magnetic field B అక్కడి నుండే వస్తుంది.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The field contribution dB points in the same direction as the current element dl — along the wire.
trigger_phrases: dB in the direction of the current

epic_c_branches.misconception: Every current element of the wire contributes the same amount of field at P, regardless of its angle or distance.
trigger_phrases: all pieces give the same field
angle does not matter for dB
each dl adds the same amount

epic_c_branches.misconception: The Biot-Savart law and Ampere's law give different values for the magnetic field of a straight wire.
trigger_phrases: Biot-Savart gives a different answer than Ampere
summing dB does not give mu0 I over 2 pi r

epic_c_branches.misconception: The current element dl in the Biot-Savart law means the entire wire, not an infinitesimal piece.
trigger_phrases: what is dl, the whole wire?

epic_c_branches.misconception: The field contribution dB points straight from the element toward P, along r-hat (radially).

STATE_8.label: Why the far ends barely matter

STATE_10.label: Free explore — sliders + rotate
STATE_10.formula_overlay: dB = (μ₀/4π) I dl sinθ / r²   ·   r = d/sinθ   ·   B = μ₀I/2πd

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the Biot-Savart law when they can state that it gives the magnetic field dB of a SINGLE infinitesimal current element dl (not the whole wire), that the magnitude is dB = (μ₀/4π) I dl sinθ/r² so the contribution grows with current and element length, grows with sinθ, and falls as the inverse square of distance, that the DIRECTION is dl × r̂ — perpendicular to both the element and the line to P, tangent to the circle and never along the wire or radially toward P — that current out of the page (·) circulates the field anti-clockwise while current into the page (⊗) circulates it clockwise, that the TOTAL field at any point is the vector sum (integral) of every element's dB so that summing over an infinite straight wire reproduces exactly B = μ₀I/2πr (identical to Ampere's law), and that distant elements contribute negligibly because their sinθ tends to zero. (The finite-wire result B = (μ₀I/4πr)(sinφ₁+sinφ₂) and on-axis loop fields are downstream applications, not assessed by this conceptual V1 quiz.)

questions.stem: In the Biot-Savart law dB = (μ₀/4π) I(dl × r̂)/r², what does the term 'dl' (the current element) refer to?

options.A: A very short piece of the wire, of length dl, carrying the current I — the total field is found by adding the contributions of all such pieces
options.B: The entire length of the wire, treated as one single source of field
options.C: The distance from the wire to the point P where the field is being measured
options.D: The small change in the current as you move along the wire

questions.tested_idea: dl is one infinitesimal current-carrying element of the wire; the total field is the sum over all such elements.
questions.parallel_form_stem: When the Biot-Savart law is applied to a bent or curved wire, what does the quantity dl stand for in the expression for dB?

questions.stem: A straight wire carries current I and P is a fixed point. Element X is directly across from P (angle θ = 90°, smallest distance r); element Y of the same length is far along the wire (θ ≈ 10°, much larger distance). Which produces the larger field dB at P, and why?

options.A: X, because dB ∝ sinθ/r²: X has the largest sinθ and the smallest r, while Y's small angle and greater distance both shrink its contribution
options.B: They contribute equally, because both elements have the same length and carry the same current I
options.C: Y, because it is farther from P, and a greater distance always means a stronger magnetic field
options.D: X, but only because it is closer — the angle θ has no effect on the size of dB

questions.tested_idea: An element's field contribution scales as sinθ/r², so the element across from P (θ=90°, smallest r) dominates and far small-angle elements add little.
questions.parallel_form_stem: For a point P beside a long wire, compare the field contributions of the element nearest P and an element near the far end of the wire. Which is larger, and why?

questions.stem: By the Biot-Savart law, in which direction does the field contribution dB at point P point?

options.A: Perpendicular to BOTH the element dl and the line to P, given by dl × r̂ — tangent to the circle through P
options.B: Along the wire, in the same direction as the current element dl
options.C: Straight from the element toward P, pointing along r̂
options.D: Always vertically upward, no matter where P is located

questions.tested_idea: dB is perpendicular to both dl and r̂ (the cross product dl × r̂) — tangent to the circle, never along the wire and never radial.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Using the Biot-Savart law, what is the direction of the magnetic field contribution dB produced at a nearby point P by a single current element?

questions.stem: A current flows straight OUT of the page (shown by a dot ⊙). For a point P directly to the RIGHT of the wire, which way does the magnetic field point?

options.A: Upward — the field circulates anti-clockwise around current that comes out of the page, so on the right side it is tangent and points up
options.B: To the right, straight away from the wire toward P
options.C: Out of the page, in the same direction the dot represents
options.D: Downward — current coming out of the page circulates clockwise

questions.tested_idea: Current out of the page (⊙) circulates the field anti-clockwise; using dl × r̂ the field is tangent (up on the right side), never radial.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A wire carries current INTO the page (marked ⊗). For a point directly to the LEFT of the wire, which way does the magnetic field point?

questions.stem: You integrate the Biot-Savart contributions dB from every element of an infinite straight wire. What total field B do you get at perpendicular distance r?

options.A: Exactly B = μ₀I/2πr — the same circular field Ampere's law gives; the result emerges from summing all the elements
options.B: A different, larger value than Ampere's law gives, because Biot-Savart counts every element separately
options.C: Zero, because the contributions from elements above and below P cancel each other out
options.D: A field that points along the wire, since all the dl elements point along the wire

questions.tested_idea: Summing every dB from the Biot-Savart law over the infinite wire yields exactly B = μ₀I/2πr, identical to Ampere's law — the circle emerges from the sum.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Integrating the Biot-Savart contributions of all elements of a long straight wire, what magnetic field do you obtain at perpendicular distance r, and how does it compare with Ampere's law?

questions.stem: Why does an infinitely long wire give almost the same field at a nearby point P as a wire that is merely very long?

options.A: Because each element's contribution ∝ sinθ/r²: the distant elements nearly line up with r̂ (sinθ → 0) and are far away, so they add almost nothing
options.B: Because the distant elements actually contribute the most, but they cancel out in pairs
options.C: Because every element contributes equally, so adding a few more elements at the ends changes nothing noticeably
options.D: Because the magnetic field does not depend on the length of the wire at all, only on the current

questions.tested_idea: Distant elements contribute negligibly because sinθ → 0 (and r is large), so a very long wire behaves almost like an infinite one.
questions.parallel_form_stem: As a wire is made longer and longer, why does the field at a nearby point P stop increasing noticeably once the wire is already long?
